rationale: The two papers both involve dynamic Bayesian modeling in advertising, but their substantive settings differ markedly: the citing paper focuses on Bayesian nonparametric state-space methods for weekly awareness/GRP and wear-out models, while the cited paper focuses on emotion, eye-tracking, and zapping in internet video ads. With only a DOI-level match and no in-text citation context or reference text, there is insufficient evidence to confidently classify this as clearly incidental or clearly central.
